pattern: A grid traversal from the start cell recolors every orthogonally connected pixel sharing the original color, the classic flood fill.
hints: Only pixels reachable from the start through neighbors of the same original color should change.
Capture the starting color first, then explore outward to its same-colored neighbors.
Guard against an infinite loop when the new color equals the original color, and recolor each visited cell as you go.

edge_inputs.description: new color equals original, no infinite loop
fill only the connected region not all same-colored

stress.description: random small grid of a few colors with random start and new color
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    rows = rng.randint(1, 8)
    cols = rng.randint(1, 8)
    image = [[rng.randint(0, 3) for _ in range(cols)] for _ in range(rows)]
    sr = rng.randint(0, rows - 1)
    sc = rng.randint(0, cols - 1)
    color = rng.randint(0, 5)
    return [image, sr, sc, color]
